title: Ken Goldberg Keynote Speaker: AI, Robotics, and the Future of Work
snippet: Ken Goldberg is a popular public speaker on AI and robotics, an award-winning roboticist, filmmaker, and artist, and the William S. Floyd Distinguished Chair in Engineering at UC Berkeley. In this talk, he discusses Moravec's paradox, the challenges of robot grasping, the potential 'ChatGPT moment' for robotics, and how physical intelligence will complement artificial intelligence to address labor
match_reason: social (video) — This is a recorded keynote presentation delivered by Ken Goldberg himself, focusing on AI, robotics, and physical intelligence.

title: Ken Goldberg - ICRA 2026 - Real2Render2Real
snippet: Ken Goldberg presents at the ICRA 2026 Workshop on Generative Digital Twins for Real2Sim and Sim2Real Transfer in Robotics. He discusses taking a real demonstration, extracting a 3D model, rendering variations to train a policy, and running it back on a real robot, alongside digital twins of plants.
match_reason: social (video) — Ken Goldberg is the featured speaker presenting his lab's research on Real2Render2Real and digital twins.

title: SherAgent: Scaling Attack Investigation in the Wild via LLM-Empowered Iterative Query-Filter Backtracking
snippet: Provenance-based attack investigation enables viable automation by standardizing data and query logic; however, it is critically hindered in practice by dependency explosions and fragmented causal chains in the wild. Motivated by these findings, we propose SherAgent, an LLM-empowered automated investigation system.
match_reason: websearch (blog_post) — Giovanni Vigna is a co-author of this paper introducing an LLM-empowered automated attack investigation system, published on arXiv on July 10, 2026.

title: California Assembly Higher Education Committee Adopts SCR 82 to Coordinate Public Higher Ed AI Policies
snippet: The Assembly Higher Education Committee adopted SCR 82, a resolution urging California's public higher education segments—the University of California, California State University, and California Community Colleges—to convene a joint workgroup to establish unified best practices for AI use.
match_reason: websearch (news_article) — Directly impacts the UC system by urging the UC President to collaborate with CSU and CCC leaders to establish intersegmental AI policies and guidelines.

title: California State University Board of Trustees Tracks SCR 82 Progress in July 2026 Legislative Update
snippet: In its July 2026 legislative tracking report, the CSU Board of Trustees highlighted the status of Senate Concurrent Resolution 82 (Niello). The resolution encourages the leaders of the UC, CSU, and CCC systems to form a joint workgroup to review and report on AI usage in higher education.
match_reason: websearch (position_statement) — Provides a July 2026 status update on California state-level legislative efforts to mandate intersegmental AI policy coordination across the UC and CSU systems.
